items.title: Banks Kick Off Earnings Season On Wednesday: Retail Traders Bet Big On Financials Ahead Of Regulatory Tailwinds
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: Banks are expected to report strong Q4 earnings, with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan Chase, and Citigroup projected to post double-digit EPS growth. Retail investors have been bullish on financial stocks ahead of earnings, anticipating positive regulatory changes under the new administration.

items.title: Wall Street Rebounds As Producer Inflation Misses Estimates, Tesla Gains, Chinese Stocks Rally
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: Wall Street rebounded as December's producer prices came in below expectations, easing inflation concerns and boosting hopes for potential Fed policy easing. Tesla surged on a bullish note from Morgan Stanley, while Chinese stocks rallied on regulatory support.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Chinese stocks rallied on regulatory support, with Alibaba advancing 2%.
Chinese stocks rallied on regulatory support, with Baidu advancing 2.4%.
Small caps led the charge, with the iShares Russell 2000 ETF up 0.9%, as traders rotated into economic-sensitive stocks.
Micron Technology gained along with other semiconductor stocks as the iShares Semiconductor ETF climbed nearly 1%.
NVIDIA rose more than 2% along with other semiconductor stocks.
Chinese stocks rallied on regulatory support, with PDD Holdings rising over 3%.
Palantir Technologies gained along with other tech stocks.
The S&P 500, tracked by the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, gained 0.5%.
Tesla surged 3% after Morgan Stanley raised its price target, citing growth in robotaxi and rideshare services.
